steps.title: Identify the generational reversal
steps.content: The passage states that children don't inherit the trait, then shifts to say grandchildren could inherit it. This unexpected recovery after a skipped generation requires a contrast transition to signal the reversal.

steps.title: Apply the contrast
steps.content:
”Nonetheless” (meaning “despite that”) perfectly bridges this: despite children not inheriting the trait, grandchildren could still inherit it from the grandparent.

The other three options—”Specifically,” “Besides,” and “In addition”—add information or narrow the scope rather than signal a reversal.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
